title: Rocky
commentary: {"overall": "Broke club fighter; the grind IS the movie; goal is 'going the distance' not winning or riches; Adrian romance tender and mutual.", "wealth": "Broke club fighter; the grind is the film", "agency": "Goes the distance by choice", "core": "Self-respect over victory", "music": "Gonna Fly Now", "inclusion": "Band 5/10 (neutral): working-class Philadelphia; Apollo an equal; neutral", "romance": "Adrian, tender and mutual"}

title: American History X
commentary: {"overall": "Working-class family; hate and deradicalization ARE the text; transformation through chosen confrontation with his own beliefs; brutal honest consequences.", "wealth": "Working-class family", "agency": "Chooses transformation in prison", "core": "Hate and its unlearning", "music": "Score", "inclusion": "Band 10/10 (inclusion is the lesson): racism and deradicalization are the text", "romance": "None central"}

title: Shawshank Redemption
commentary: {"overall": "Ordinary man stripped of everything; escape earned through two decades of quiet depicted work - the ultimate grind; hope and friendship core is elite; no romance.", "wealth": "Everything stripped; escape earned over decades", "agency": "Hope as a discipline", "core": "Hope, friendship, institutionalization", "music": "Score; one Mozart scene", "inclusion": "Band 6/10 (neutral): Red and the prison's shared humanity; neutral-plus", "romance": "None"}

title: Parasite
commentary: {"overall": "Class critique IS the film; aspiration to wealth is punished and the system itself indicted; nobody glorified; othering-by-smell is inclusion examined.", "wealth": "Class critique is the film; aspiration punished", "agency": "The Kims scheme brilliantly, then it collapses", "core": "Dignity and the smell of poverty", "music": "Score", "inclusion": "Band 7/10 (representation, not the lesson): othering-by-smell examined; class contempt made visible", "romance": "Peripheral"}

title: Trainspotting
commentary: {"overall": "Working-class Edinburgh; addiction rendered with honesty; 'choose life' irony; legendary soundtrack; agency compromised by the subject itself.", "wealth": "Working-class Edinburgh", "agency": "Addiction compromises choice; Renton chooses life, ambiguously", "core": "Addiction without varnish", "music": "Legendary soundtrack", "inclusion": "Band 4/10 (homogeneous default / missed opportunity): underclass interiority is real but the world is homogeneous and the Diane subplot ages badly", "romance": "The Diane subplot is uncomfortable"}

title: There Will Be Blood
commentary: {"overall": "Self-made grind depicted BUT wealth accumulation as total soul-destruction - cinema's harshest ambition critique; isolation as the wage of greed.", "wealth": "Self-made grind as soul-destruction; the harshest critique", "agency": "Plainview wills everything into being", "core": "Ambition's wage is isolation", "music": "Greenwood score", "inclusion": "Band 4/10 (homogeneous default / missed opportunity): misanthropic and homogeneous by design; nothing taught", "romance": "None"}

title: The Big Lebowski
commentary: {"overall": "The Dude is the anti-wealth protagonist; the millionaire is a FRAUD (borrowed status, embezzled money); abiding over striving; low agency - things happen to him.", "wealth": "The Dude is anti-wealth; the millionaire is a fraud", "agency": "Things happen to him; he abides", "core": "Abiding as philosophy, loosely", "music": "Dream-sequence soundtrack", "inclusion": "Band 5/10 (neutral): neutral", "romance": "Maude is transactional; odd"}

title: Pulp Fiction
commentary: {"overall": "Working criminals, no inherited status; Jules's redemption thread carries the core; iconic needle drops; morally chaotic but status-indifferent.", "wealth": "Working criminals; status-indifferent", "agency": "Jules chooses to walk the earth", "core": "One redemption thread carries it", "music": "Iconic needle drops", "inclusion": "Band 3/10 (harmful othering present): casual slurs and racialized menace present in the text, played for cool", "romance": "The Mia chapter; moderate"}

title: Titanic
commentary: {"overall": "Class is the architecture: steerage vs first class, Cal is wealth-as-villainy, Rose REJECTS the gilded cage; romance central but she ultimately saves herself.", "wealth": "Class is the architecture; Rose rejects the gilded cage", "agency": "Rose chooses, twice", "core": "Freedom versus suffocation", "music": "Horner, and that song", "inclusion": "Band 6/10 (neutral): steerage humanized against first-class contempt; class empathy without a full lesson", "romance": "Central romance; she saves herself"}

title: Fight Club
commentary: {"overall": "Anti-consumerism as thesis ('things you own end up owning you'); identity critique; docked on inclusion (insular masculinity) and romance framing.", "wealth": "Anti-consumerism as thesis", "agency": "The unreliable narrator undermines it", "core": "Identity and masculinity critique", "music": "Pixies finale", "inclusion": "Band 3/10 (harmful othering present): insular masculinity; Marla as a device; othering-lite present", "romance": "The Marla framing is poor"}

title: Forrest Gump
commentary: {"overall": "Ordinary man indifferent to the fortune he stumbles into ('one less thing'); luck IS the theme so agency is low; disability lead; Jenny framing mixed.", "wealth": "Indifferent to a stumbled-upon fortune", "agency": "Luck is the theme, so agency is low", "core": "Devotion and decency", "music": "Boomer jukebox", "inclusion": "Band 6/10 (neutral): a disability lead treated with dignity; Bubba; representation-forward", "romance": "The Jenny framing is mixed"}

title: The Matrix
commentary: {"overall": "Office drone but The One is the ultimate chosen-one mechanic - specialness by prophecy; reality-and-choice core; diverse cast for its era.", "wealth": "The One; specialness by prophecy", "agency": "Choice is the theme, destiny the mechanics", "core": "Reality and choice", "music": "Score plus Rage", "inclusion": "Band 6/10 (neutral): notably diverse cast for its era; neutral-plus", "romance": "A kiss revives the chosen one; inverted trope"}

title: The Dark Knight
commentary: {"overall": "Billionaire hero whose power is inherited fortune and purchased hardware; ethics-of-escalation core is real; wealth unexamined as the enabling condition.", "wealth": "Billionaire hero; wealth the unexamined enabler", "agency": "Chooses to take the blame", "core": "Escalation ethics, real", "music": "Zimmer score", "inclusion": "Band 5/10 (neutral): neutral", "romance": "The Rachel triangle is thin"}

title: American Gangster
commentary: {"overall": "Rise-to-riches crime ascension rendered seductively before the fall; discipline depicted but the empire is the aspiration.", "wealth": "Crime ascension rendered seductively", "agency": "Discipline depicted on both sides", "core": "Two ambitions mirrored", "music": "Score plus soul", "inclusion": "Band 4/10 (homogeneous default / missed opportunity): Harlem context gestured at; violence glamor without community interiority", "romance": "Peripheral"}

title: The Godfather
commentary: {"overall": "Born into a crime dynasty - succession IS the plot; power inherited by blood; tragedy-framed but aesthetically glorified; women as acquisitions or exclusions.", "wealth": "A crime dynasty; succession by blood", "agency": "Michael chooses his damnation", "core": "Family, and the corrosion of a soul", "music": "Iconic score", "inclusion": "Band 2/10 (harmful othering present): women as acquisitions and exclusions — Kay shut out behind a literal closing door; the insularity is celebrated as code", "romance": "Wives as acquisitions"}

title: Goodfellas
commentary: {"overall": "'As far back as I can remember I always wanted to be a gangster' - status ascension as seduction; punished ending but the glamour is what lingers; women sidelined.", "wealth": "Ascension as seduction; the glamour outlives the fall", "agency": "Henry rides it, then rats", "core": "The life as addiction", "music": "Perfect needle drops", "inclusion": "Band 2/10 (harmful othering present): women sidelined into complicity or decoration; the ethnic insularity romanticized", "romance": "Karen complicit, then sidelined"}

title: Scarface
commentary: {"overall": "The ur wealth-ascension fantasy - The World Is Yours; cautionary ending the culture famously ignored in favour of the poster; excess IS the appeal.", "wealth": "The World Is Yours; the culture kept the poster", "agency": "Tony takes everything", "core": "Excess is the appeal", "music": "Push It to the Limit", "inclusion": "Band 2/10 (harmful othering present): cartoonish Cuban-immigrant stereotyping; a subculture rendered as violent spectacle without interiority", "romance": "Elvira as trophy"}

title: The Wolf of Wall Street
commentary: {"overall": "Three hours of wealth porn with satirical intent that plays as aspiration; women as commodities throughout; the rubric's adult-world floor.", "wealth": "Wealth porn playing as aspiration", "agency": "Jordan cons everyone, including the audience", "core": "Nothing under the excess", "music": "Needle drops", "inclusion": "Band 0/10 (active degradation as text): women as commodities is the active, sustained text — bought, ranked, traded on screen as spectacle; the definitional floor", "romance": "Trophy wives; zero"}
